# PART I — FINAL (RED-TEAMED) MASTER PLAN

# FINAL MASTER PLAN
## *When Does Deep Learning Actually Help Crime Forecasting — and Can We Trust Its Uncertainty When the Records Are Biased?*
### A Rigorous Benchmark, an Anchored Correction Pipeline, and an Honest Impossibility Result

*Research-director final revision. This version survives both red-team passes. It reorders the program so the unscoopable, correct piece leads; demotes every scooped primitive to a cited application; withdraws the two theorems that contained outright errors under real-data conditions; and states every remaining guarantee at exactly the strength its assumptions support — no stronger. Where a guarantee is about the anchor's measurement rather than latent truth, the theorem now says so.*

---

## 0. What changed, and why (the executive summary of the revision)

The prior plan led with a "novel latent-coverage theorem." Both reviewers converged on the same verdict: that headline is a **composition of four 2022–2025 papers** (Csillag conformal-PPI 2025; Jin–Ren–Candès conformal sensitivity 2023; Kluger clustered-PPI 2025; Chugg auditing-by-betting 2023), and its central guarantee is **unfalsifiable in the exact co-bias regime the paper is about**. Meanwhile the piece I ranked *last* — the honest sparsity-stratified benchmark — is the one thing no one has published and no one can scoop.

So the program is **inverted and hardened**:

1. **Lead with the benchmark (was S3, now the headline).** It is a measurement standard, immune to novelty attack, and it is the correct primary contribution.
2. **Demote the anchored PPI-conformal pipeline to a *demonstrated method within the benchmark*,** with all four primitives cited as prior art and Layer C explicitly labeled "Jin–Ren–Candès sensitivity analysis applied to recording propensity."
3. **Keep only the correct *negative/impossibility* results as theory:** κ is not point-identified (the slope non-cancellation), records ≠ latent, and the dependence-free union bound. These are correct, defensible, and lead the theory section.
4. **Withdraw the two constructive theorems that were wrong under real conditions:** the closed-form κ ratio (Eq. 5 — FE/level mismatch, staggered-DiD negative weights) and the PPI "never worse than anchor" claim (fails under non-random labeling). They return only in weakened, correctly-derived form.
5. **Restate every latent-coverage guarantee as coverage of *the anchor's estimand*, with anchor-independence as a named in-theorem assumption**, not a footnote.

The result is a smaller, honest, genuinely publishable applied program. It is **not** a NeurIPS-main methods contribution, and the plan now says so plainly in §7.

---

## 1. Final Title + Thesis

**Title:** *Records Are Not Ground Truth: A Sparsity-Stratified Benchmark for Probabilistic Crime Forecasting, with Anchored Uncertainty Correction and an Honest Account of What a Detection Shock Can and Cannot Identify.*

**Thesis (three claims, in priority order):**

1. **Empirical (the lead).** On real Chicago + NYC data, deep spatiotemporal probabilistic forecasters must be scored against seasonal-naive and historical-average with **CRPSS stratified by count sparsity**, and against an **independent victimization anchor for coverage**, not against the recorded label alone. When you do this, deep models win only in dense, high-count strata and *lose to trivial baselines in the sparse tail* — and existing "coverage guarantees" certify the biased record, not the truth. This is a reusable community standard, and it reframes a decade of over-claimed crime-forecasting results.

2. **Methodological (the demonstration).** Correcting records toward a latent victimization process is possible *only* by importing an independent measurement channel; we assemble the correction from existing tools (prediction-powered inference + conformal sensitivity analysis + group-conditional conformal) and show, via a pre-registered falsification protocol, where it works and where anchor bias silently defeats it.

3. **Theoretical (the impossibility, honestly).** A detection shock (ShotSpotter) does **not** point-identify the observation-feedback gain κ — the shock moves a *slope* on the unobserved latent log-intensity, so the difference-in-differences carries an irreducible latent-level term. We prove the non-identification, give the one object that *is* identified (a relative amplification ratio), and provide correctly-derived sensitivity bounds. We do not claim to have solved the feedback loop; we characterize precisely why it cannot be solved from records alone.

The honest one-liner: **"valid, falsifiable uncertainty *where the anchor supports it*, and a clean impossibility result *where it does not* — not a solution to predictive-policing feedback."**

---

## 2. Final Contribution List (with defensible novelty deltas)

Each contribution states its **delta over the specific paper that would otherwise scoop it**, and its **honest strength**.

### C1 (HEADLINE) — The sparsity-stratified CRPSS + latent-coverage benchmark
- **What it is:** the first probabilistic crime-forecasting benchmark that (i) scores CRPS/CRPSS *stratified by count sparsity*, (ii) includes real ARIMA/seasonal-naive/historical-average lower bounds, (iii) reports coverage against an *independent* victimization anchor, not the recorded label, and (iv) audits per-area coverage equity.
- **Delta:** UQGNN (2025), STZINB (KDD 2022), STMGNN-ZINB (2024) all report CRPS **on the observed label, unstratified, no anchor**. No prior benchmark separates "wins on dense cells" from "loses to `median()` on sparse cells," and none validates coverage against an external channel. This is not a new layer — it is a measurement standard, and that is precisely why it cannot be scooped.
- **Strength: HIGH.** Immune to the novelty attack. Durable community value. This is the paper's spine.

### C2 (SUPPORTING) — Impossibility of point-identifying the feedback gain from a detection shock
- **What it is:** the exact non-cancellation result. Under a slope-shifting shock, DiD $=(A_1-A_0)\,\ell_{\mathcal T,1}$; the treated-post latent level $\ell_{\mathcal T,1}$ does not difference away, so κ is not point-identified from records + DiD. Only the *ratio* $A_0/A_1$ is identified.
- **Delta:** this is the *correct* version of Project A's false Theorem 3. It obeys — rather than contradicts — Knox–Lowe–Mummolo (2020), selective labels (Lakkaraju 2017), Moreno–Girón (1998). The constructive point-ID is retracted; the impossibility is the contribution. Fe (2024, JQC) partial-identifies the dark figure from *survey* moments; ours is the *DiD-moment* analogue and is explicitly framed as weaker/complementary, not a new bound.
- **Strength: MEDIUM-HIGH as a negative result.** Correct, defensible, and it disarms the econometrics referee by making his objection our theorem.

### C3 (SUPPORTING) — The anchored correction pipeline, as a demonstrated application
- **What it is:** an assembled pipeline — Pogit/thinning reporting model → reweighted (AIPW) PPI for the population latent rate → group-conditional conformal sensitivity intervals for the per-area latent rate → Dijkstra/Pareto routing over those intervals → anytime-valid monitor.
- **Delta (stated honestly):** every primitive is prior art — PPI++/cross-PPI (Angelopoulos 2023; Zrnić–Candès 2024); conformal-powered PPI (Csillag 2025); conformal sensitivity analysis (**Jin–Ren–Candès, PNAS 2023 — Layer C is their method applied to recording propensity**); clustered/spatial PPI (Kluger 2025; Spatially-Robust PPI 2026); group-conditional conformal (Romano 2020; POGO 2026); betting confidence sequences (Waudby-Smith–Ramdas 2024) inside auditing-by-betting (Chugg 2023). **The novelty is the domain assembly + the falsification protocol, not any method.** We claim it as an applied-track contribution and nothing more.
- **Strength: MEDIUM as application, LOW as method.** This is what makes the paper KDD-ADS/FAccT-shaped rather than a workshop note.

### C4 (SUPPORTING) — The falsification protocol itself (E1–E6 with pre-registered kill criteria)
- **What it is:** a reusable protocol for testing whether a *latent-correction* claim is real or circular, including the E5 blind semi-synthetic non-circularity harness and the E2 natural-experiment placebo.
- **Delta:** Project A's own Theorem 2 conceded latent coverage was unfalsifiable on passive data; this operationalizes falsifiability *conditional on an anchor*, and — critically — states in the protocol itself that E2 is **necessary but not sufficient** (a trivial dummy-regression corrector also removes a known discontinuity), so level-validity requires the independent level anchor.
- **Strength: MEDIUM.** Methodologically honest; pairs with C1 as the "how to evaluate" half of the benchmark.

**Dropped entirely** (were "contributions," now deleted or cited-only):
- The "novel latent-coverage theorem" as a headline → reframed as C3, cited to Csillag + Jin–Ren–Candès.
- The routing "propagation theorem" ($(1-\alpha^*)^K$ and even the $1-K_g\alpha$ "crown jewel") → it is the union bound; kept as a correctly-stated *lemma with post-selection caveats*, not a contribution.
- The anytime-valid monitor as a contribution → engineering feature (Chugg 2023 on a relabeled residual), with honest detection-latency disclosure.
- C2's $1/(1-\kappa)$ amplification "law" → cited identity (Glaeser–Sacerdote–Scheinkman social multiplier).
- CTC-Op "differentiable Routine Activity Theory" → optional interpretable layer, no claim.
- All 7 + 7 patent claims → at most one narrow system claim (§7).

## 3. Final Pipeline (buildable, with every fix wired in)

Legend: **[A:file]** salvaged from CIVIC-SAFE, **[B:file]** from CTC-HSTGT, **[NEW]** to build, **[DEL]** delete.

**Stage 0 — Substrate & anchors.**
- **[A:build_monthly_panel]** (unit×month, structural zeros, per-100k, log1p) — keep verbatim; it's the sound shared core.
- **[NEW]** anchor ingestion: NCVS/CSEW small-area victimization **with the explicit understanding that these are model-based SAE estimates, not direct measurements** (O1.3), carrying their design variances *and* a flag for which ACS covariates they use (so we can detect overlap with GNN features); 911 Calls-for-Service as the fine-grained second channel; reporting-rate natural experiments (NCRS-2002, portal rollouts, verified CPD ShotSpotter deployment) held out for falsification only.
- **[DEL]** silent synthetic fallback **[B:pipeline.py:78–81]**; **[DEL]** `np.random.uniform` missing-feature fills **[B:causal_model.py:30–35]** → hard-fail.
- Every anchor tagged with native resolution (MSA/PFA × quarter); this tag is **enforced** downstream and is the reason the latent claims are scoped coarse (O7).

**Stage 1 — Honest count backbone (prerequisite, not a contribution).**
- **[DEL]** 3-class head **[B:num_risk_levels:3]**; **[DEL]** hardcoded-Granger matrix; **[DEL]** Δ=0.014 "emergence."
- **[NEW]** genuine ZINB/NB head with dispersion + CQR conditional quantiles; Gaussian-copula coupling over (violence, property, disorder) with learnable correlation — the real version of B's vaporware.
- Backbone: GATv2 + causal-transformer **[A]** *or* lean STZINB encoder — whichever wins the CRPSS gate. No architecture novelty claimed.
- **[B:ctc_operator.py]** retained only as optional interpretable sign-constrained Offender×Target×(1−Guardian) layer; false gradient math deleted; supervised on *real exogenous covariates*, never self-referential means.
- **Hard gate K2:** must beat seasonal-naive AND historical-average in CRPSS on dense strata before any interval claim attaches.

**Stage 2 — Reporting model + reweighted PPI (T1), corrected per O4.1.**
- Measurement model $Y_i\mid\theta_i,\pi_i\sim\text{Poisson}(\pi_i\theta_i)$; π modeled as endogenous to past predictions (feedback = modeled, not identified).
- $\hat\pi$ via Pogit/binomial-thinning GLM on gold set $L$; optional fine-resolution identification from $(Y_i,R_i)$ under Hu–Schennach rank condition.
- **[NEW] AIPW/reweighted cross-PPI** with an explicit *labeling-propensity model* (which MSAs are surveyed is non-random) — **not** vanilla PPI++, whose "never worse" guarantee is false under this shift (O4.1). Efficiency claim restated as "never worse asymptotically *if labeling weights are consistent*."

**Stage 3 — Group-conditional conformal sensitivity intervals (the demonstration), corrected per O1.**
- Work at **rate** level; base score CQR-on-ZINB mapped $q^\theta=q^Y/\hat\pi$. **[DEL]** `1−probs[label]` and `std·1.96`.
- **Weighted split-conformal** (not plain) to handle the deployment-vs-anchor covariate shift; population is stated as **the anchor sampling distribution** (O1.1).
- Survey-noise handling: because scores are heteroskedastic after per-cell deconvolution, we **normalize scores first, then conformalize** (fixes O1.1.3), and correct the union bookkeeping to $1-\alpha-(n{+}1)\delta$ or use $\delta'=\delta/(n{+}1)$ (O1.2).
- Per-area equity via Mondrian using **[A:ECRCCalibrator]**, with O6.2 dependence-robust (blocked) concentration replacing plain Hoeffding, and an **explicit statement that for small $n_a$ there is no finite-sample per-area guarantee** — shrinkage trades coverage for variance, so per-area coverage is a *monitored diagnostic*, not a theorem (O6.1).
- **Layer C = Jin–Ren–Candès conformal sensitivity** applied to the recording-odds Rosenbaum ball; report both $\Gamma^*$ **and interval width at the survey-implied $\Gamma_{\text{data}}$** (O1.4), because if $\Gamma_{\text{data}}$ is large the robust intervals are honestly uninformative.
- **[A:should_abstain]** retained for the runaway pole.

**Stage 4 — Partial-ID of the gain (the impossibility result), corrected per O2.**
- Reduced-form DiD via **[A:estimate_did]** gated by **[A:event_study]** pre-trends, on **verified CPD ShotSpotter deployment** (district × activation month, staggered).
- **[DEL]** `identify_kappa_did` point-ID and `rho_hat = tau/shock_log`.
- Estimator re-derived so numerator and denominator share the **same FE transformation** (O2.1); staggered slope-change handled with **Callaway–Sant'Anna / de Chaisemartin–D'Haultfœuille group-time estimands** to avoid negative-weight bias (O2.4); centering $c_t$ defined on the **count scale** (not the lognormal Jensen correction, invalid under zero-inflation, O2.5).
- Deliverable relabeled **"sharp within the declared sensitivity rectangle"**, not Manski-sharp (O2.6). Weak-ID pole reported in the FE metric with Anderson–Rubin intervals; **honest expectation: on the real null instrument (τ≈−0.04, p≈0.069) the bounds include zero.**

**Stage 5 — Routing (application layer, union-bound lemma only).**
- **[A:DijkstraRouter]**; **[DEL]** "Tsinghua/Duan SSSP" branding.
- BOA* Pareto over conformal interval edge-costs; **[DEL]** $(1-\alpha^*)^K$.
- Safety stated as the **dependence-free union bound** $1-K\alpha$ (one-sided; specify tail bookkeeping, O3.2), with an explicit **post-selection caveat**: the bound is valid for a *fixed* path; for the adaptively-chosen safest route we either union over the graph edges ($1-|E|\alpha$) or use a selection-aware construction (O3.3). Group estimand defined precisely (origin/destination/traversed, O3.4). This is a lemma, not a contribution.

**Stage 6 — Anytime-valid monitor (engineering feature, honest cadence).**
- **[NEW]** betting CS on the corrected residual, **with the filtration explicitly including logged deployed predictions** so the supermartingale property holds under feedback (O5.1) — this *requires* prospective prediction logging, stated as a deployment precondition.
- Disclose **detection latency of years** because the anchor (NCVS) arrives annually (O5.2); sold as a governance safeguard, never as real-time.

**Stage 7 — Equity audit + fairness posture.**
- **[A:BAS/DAD]** kept; fix `AnomalySkillCoefficient` variance-ratio bug.
- **Do NOT headline "equalized coverage across race"** (Corbett-Davies 2023; "conformal sets cause disparate impact" 2024). Headline *calibrated group risk of the latent process* + explicitly-defended thresholds; coverage-equity is a monitored diagnostic. Cite Chouldechova (2017) impossibility.

## 4. Final Corrected Theorems (with exact assumptions and honest scope)

Only three formal results survive. Two are impossibility/dependence-free (strong); one is coverage-of-the-anchor (correctly scoped).

**Theorem 1 (Non-identification of the feedback gain — the headline theory).**
Let the log-linear reduced form be $y_{it}=A(\kappa_{it})x_{it}+f_i+g_t+\varepsilon_{it}$, $A(\kappa)=1/(1-\kappa)$, with a shock changing $\kappa$ (a slope) on treated-post. Under no-anticipation, exogenous shock, and parallel latent trends,
$$\theta_{\text{DiD}}=(A_1-A_0)\,\ell_{\mathcal T,1},$$
where $\ell_{\mathcal T,1}=\mathbb E[x\mid \mathcal T,\text{post}]$ is the **unobserved** treated-post latent log-level. Hence κ is **not** point-identified from records + DiD. The only identified object is the ratio $A_0/A_1=1-\theta_{\text{DiD}}/\bar y_{\mathcal T,1}$, **provided the same FE transformation is applied to numerator and denominator** (else the ratio is biased by the between/within decomposition of treated latent intensity).
*Status: correct, defensible, disarms the econometrics referee.*

**Theorem 2 (Sensitivity bounds on κ — correctly labeled).**
Under Theorem 1's assumptions plus a *declared rectangle* $\kappa_0\in[\underline\kappa,\overline\kappa]$, count-scale centering $c\in[\underline c,\overline c]$, and **Callaway–Sant'Anna group-time aggregation** (not TWFE, to avoid negative weights under staggered slope-change), the set
$$\Theta_{\kappa'}=\{1-(1-\hat\rho(c))(1-\kappa_0)\}$$
is the interval swept over the rectangle. It is **sharp within the declared sensitivity rectangle**, not Manski-sharp over all DGPs. Inference: Imbens–Manski, with Anderson–Rubin intervals when $|\bar y_{\mathcal T,1}|$ (in the FE metric) is small — which, we disclose, is generic in the causal specification (O2.2).
*Status: correct as a sensitivity band; expected to be wide/include zero on the real null instrument. Robustness appendix, not headline.*

**Theorem 3 (Weighted split-conformal coverage of the anchor's latent estimand).**
Let $\theta_i^{\text{svy}}$ be the anchor's (model-based) measurement of the latent rate. Under (A1) exchangeability of $\{(X_i,\theta_i^{\text{svy}})\}$ **over the anchor sampling distribution**, (A2) weighted-conformal weights consistent for the deployment-vs-anchor covariate shift, and (A3) the true recording model inside the declared Γ-ball, the Stage-3 set satisfies
$$\mathbb P\big(\theta_{n+1}^{\text{svy}}\in C_\Gamma(X_{n+1})\big)\ge 1-\alpha-(n{+}1)\delta.$$
**Named in-theorem assumption (A4, anchor independence):** coverage of the *true latent* $\theta_{n+1}$ (not the anchor's measurement) additionally requires the anchor error to be independent of the police-recording bias. **This is untestable, and it fails precisely when survey non-response co-biases with police undercount in high-disparity areas.** We therefore state the guarantee for $\theta^{\text{svy}}$ and treat coverage of true θ as *conditional on A4*, disclosed as fundamental.
*Status: correct for the anchor estimand; honestly conditional for latent truth.*

**Retracted / demoted:** the closed-form κ point estimator (Eq. 5) — withdrawn as a standalone claim, folded into Theorem 2's ratio with the FE-consistency requirement; PPI "never worse than anchor" — restated as conditional on consistent labeling weights (Stage 2); routing "theorem" — demoted to the union-bound lemma with post-selection caveat; anytime-valid "guarantee" — engineering feature conditional on prediction logging.

**Lemma (routing, honest form).** For a *fixed* path of $K$ edges with one-sided level-α upper bounds, $P(\text{all hold})\ge 1-K\alpha$, dependence-free. For an *adaptively selected* route, validity requires union over candidate edges/paths or a selection-aware construction; the naive per-path bound is post-selection-invalid.

## 5. Final Experiments, Real-Anchor Data Plan, Baselines

**Real data.** Records: Chicago 1.33M + NYC 1.51M via **[A:build_monthly_panel]**. Anchors: NCVS/CSEW (primary, coarse, SAE-based — disclosed); 911 CFS (fine, second channel); natural experiments (NCRS-2002, portal rollouts, CPD ShotSpotter) — falsification only.

**Baselines (per sparsity stratum).**
- Forecasting (CRPS/CRPSS/MPIW/calibration): seasonal-naive, historical-average, **real ARIMA** (not `median()`), STZINB-GNN, STMGNN-ZINB, UQGNN, TACTiS-2, DiffSTG. **Claim wins only where honestly won.**
- Coverage: vanilla split-conformal on records (covers the *wrong* target — that's the point), Mondrian/equalized (Romano 2020), POGO, weighted CP, CopulaCPTS, **and Jin–Ren–Candès conformal sensitivity** (the honest baseline for Layer C — we compare *against* it, not claim it).
- Latent rate: BJS/RTI small-area estimation, Wheeler–Piquero NCVS×NIBRS multipliers, **Fe (2024) dark-figure partial-ID** (the published bound Theorem 2 must be shown complementary to).
- Identification: Cheng–Long (2024), Topper (2024) ShotSpotter DiD comparators.

**Six pre-registered experiments (kill criteria K1–K3 mirror A's FINAL_PLAN).**
- **E0 (new, the true headline table): sparsity-stratified CRPSS + record-vs-anchor coverage.** Show deep models win on dense strata, lose to naive on sparse; show record-conformal "covers" but anchor-coverage fails. This is C1 and it needs no latent claim to be publishable.
- **E1 — Held-out anchor coverage.** Fit on calibration survey waves; report coverage of held-out **anchor-measured** θ + width, per group. **K1: coverage < 1−α−0.05 ⇒ retract latent-coverage claim.** *Disclosed limitation: E1 validates against the anchor and is blind to co-directional anchor bias (A4).* We state this in the caption, not hide it.
- **E2 — Natural-experiment placebo, demoted to "necessary not sufficient."** Event study on raw vs corrected across a held-out reporting shock. **K3: corrected series still shows the jump ⇒ corrector non-identifying.** We explicitly note a trivial dummy-regression corrector also passes E2, so E2 alone does not establish level-validity (cross-cutting objection).
- **E3 — Γ\* breakdown AND width at $\Gamma_{\text{data}}$.** Claim holds only if $\Gamma^*>\Gamma_{\text{data}}$; report width at $\Gamma_{\text{data}}$ so an uninformative robust interval cannot hide behind validity.
- **E4 — PPI crossover.** Show operating point past the No-Free-Lunch crossover; **honestly likely to fail at NCVS granularity** — if it does, we report PPI as below-crossover and fall back to anchor-only inference (this is a finding, not a hidden failure). Directly addresses O7.
- **E5 — Blind semi-synthetic non-circularity.** Generator with a *different functional form* than the corrector, all params withheld; measure coverage vs known synthetic θ. Proves the corrector isn't fed the generator (kills C1-circularity in a controlled setting). **Build this first.**
- **E6 — Routing under post-selection.** Verify the union-bound lemma on *fixed* routes; show the naive per-selected-path bound is optimistic (the honest correction to B's fabricated bound).

**Ablations: real retraining only. [DEL] `_simulate_ablation`.** −anchor/PPI (records-only, expected to fail E1/E2 — positive control), −Γ-layer, −Mondrian, −copula, −CTC-Op. Paired bootstrap on real predictions.

## 6. Complete Flaw-Fix Roadmap (phased; both projects)

**Phase 0 (wk 1–3) — Substrate & anchors.** Keep build_monthly_panel; build anchor ingestion with resolution tags + SAE/covariate-overlap flags; delete synthetic fallback and random-fill.

**Phase 1 (wk 3–7) — Honest backbone + CRPSS gate. *Prerequisite.*** Real ZINB+copula head; delete 3-class, hardcoded-Granger, Δ=0.014. **Pass K2 or the forecaster is a component only.** Deliver **E0** (this alone is a publishable applied paper).

**Phase 2 (wk 6–10) — Reweighted PPI + non-circularity proof.** AIPW/cross-PPI with labeling model (fixes O4.1); **build E5 first**; retract `deflate_latent_rate`.

**Phase 3 (wk 9–14) — Weighted conformal sensitivity (correctly scoped).** CQR-on-ZINB rate score; weighted split-conformal; normalized-then-conformalized scores; δ/(n+1) bookkeeping; Mondrian via ECRC with blocked concentration; Layer C = Jin–Ren–Candès applied; report Γ\* and width at Γ_data. Run E1, E3, E4. Enforce K1. Delete invalid scores.

**Phase 4 (wk 12–16) — Impossibility + sensitivity bounds.** Verified CPD ShotSpotter treatment; CS/dCDH group-time estimator with FE-consistent ratio and count-scale centering; delete point-ID inversion; relabel "sharp within rectangle." Run E2. Enforce K3.

**Phase 5 (wk 15–18) — Routing.** Keep Dijkstra, drop Tsinghua branding; union-bound lemma with post-selection correction; delete $(1-\alpha^*)^K$. Run E6.

**Phase 6 (wk 17–21) — Monitor + equity.** Betting CS with prediction-logging filtration; disclose latency; BAS/DAD fixes; Corbett-Davies posture; demote 1/(1−κ) to citation.

**Phase 7 (wk 20–24) — Write-up + pre-registration.** Pre-register E0–E6 with K1–K3; cite Jin–Ren–Candès, Csillag, Kluger, Chugg, Romano, POGO, Fe, Cheng–Long, Topper, Knox–Lowe–Mummolo, Lakkaraju defensively; scope every latent claim to anchor resolution; patent only the deployed system if at all.
